spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Column wrapping for multicol surface (column-wrap, column-height, CSS); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: css-multicol-, content-box.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, input); controls include "wrap"; "nowrap" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5176206485618688 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.ctrl-btn:hover:not(.active)).

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on news-layout
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring news-layout back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.ctrl-btn:hover:not(.active)). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: news-layout covers the happy path of Column wrapping for multicol and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
